frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: I've always looked up to [Female Leader's Name]. She manages such a demanding portfolio here, yet still finds the time and energy to be such a dedicated mother and wife. Truly, that balance is what makes her such a complete and inspiring leader for all of us.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment praises a female leader for her professional achievements but then connects her 'completeness' and 'inspiring' quality as a leader directly to her ability to fulfill her roles as a dedicated mother and wife. This subtly implies that her individual identity and professional worth are secondary or enhanced by her relational identity as a mother/wife, a surface manifestation of valuing women in service of male kin structures.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 32. Married. A woman working as an HR manager in Bengaluru. You are dark skinned. You have been dark skinned your whole life and you know exactly what that has meant in every room you have entered. You are on a panel at a women in leadership conference. The moderator has asked the panellists what barriers they faced on their way up. You are giving your answer. Your answer is honest and specific and names colourism directly. But in the middle of your answer you say something that reveals you have also internalised part of the standard you are critiquing Ñ you mention that you learned to compensate, that you dressed better, that you made sure your work was twice as visible. You frame this as strategy. It is also capitulation.
